title: Sampling and  viral concentration for SARS-CoV-2 detection in wastewater
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"f1cj8","text":"This protocol has been effective at recovering enveloped viruses genes for qPCR detection, but not appropriate when infective viruses are desired.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Autoclave plastic containers and expose to UV for 10 min. 200 mL to 1 L plastic bottles with screw cap Contributed by users

Fill bottles with wastewater at sampling points using sterile gloves. Sampling points may be diverse and accessibility to water sources will be sometimes difficult depending on site infrastructure. Consider these aspects before starting sampling. If site is inaccessible by hand, use a rope tied to the bottle to reach narrow or deep places. When recovering the bottle, immediately tap it and sanitize the outside using EtOH 70%.
Place bottles in a refrigerated container (i.e. with ice flakes or gel packs) until returning to the lab. Our sampling days usually take 4-6 hours since first sample is collected.

Prepare your reagents and materials: Ultracentrifuge (Beckman Coulter, XPN-90, Your ultracentrifuge must reach 100,000 x g and cool down to 4º C) Centrifuge (Bench centrifuge, Eppendorf, 5424) 0.25 N glycine buffer (pH 9.5) Contributed by users 2× phosphate-buffered saline (PBS, pH 7.2) Contributed by users
Place 42 mL of sewage water in an appropiate ultracentrifugation tube and ultracentrifugate as follows: 100000 x g, °C, 4 °C 01:00:00
Discard supernatant resuspend pelleted viral particles as follows:
Resuspend viral particles in 3.5 mL, glycine buffer .
Add 3.5 mL, PBS and gently mix.
Clarify supernatant by centrifugation as follows: 12000 x g, °C, 00:15:00
Finally recover viruses by ultracentrifugation as follows: 100000 x g, °C, 4 °C 01:00:00
Process immediately for nucleic acid extraction or store at -80 °C .
creator: Iraola Lab
